compiled_state.title: GREEN: Implement tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh — full PR lifecycle E2E
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Story DD Coverage
This task is responsible for satisfying:
- DD1: E2E test creates a test branch, pushes a change, creates a PR, verifies CI status checks appear (all required contexts post a conclusion — not pending/expected), asserts the PR cannot merge until checks pass (Ruleset blocks on failure), and asserts a conforming branch merges successfully after checks pass
- DD2: E2E test cleans up all created GitHub resources (test branch, test PR) in a finally/cleanup block, even on test failure

## Implementation Notes
Create tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh following the pattern from
tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app-real-url.sh exactly.

### Opt-in gate (required):
```bash
if [ "${RUN_CI_E2E:-}" != "1" ]; then
    echo "SKIP: RUN_CI_E2E not set to 1. ..."
    echo "PASSED: 0  FAILED: 0  (skipped)"
    exit 0
fi
```

### Env prereq checks (required, exit 2 on failure):
- gh CLI: command -v gh || { echo "ERROR: gh not found"; exit 2; }
- CI_E2E_REPO: non-empty, format owner/repo
- GH_TOKEN or existing gh auth

### Cleanup trap (required):
```bash
CLEANUP_BRANCH=""
CLEANUP_PR=""
cleanup() {
    [ -n "$CLEANUP_PR" ] && gh pr close "$CLEANUP_PR" --repo "$CI_E2E_REPO" 2>/dev/null || true
    [ -n "$CLEANUP_BRANCH" ] && git push origin --delete "$CLEANUP_BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT
```

### Phase 1 — Conforming PR:
1. Create test branch: e2e-ci-enforcement-$(date +%s)
2. Make a conforming change (touch a test-safe file like docs/ or add a blank line to a non-critical file)
3. git push origin <branch>
4. gh pr create --repo $CI_E2E_REPO --base main --head <branch> --title "E2E CI enforcement test" --body "Automated test PR"
5. Poll loop: every 30s for up to CI_E2E_TIMEOUT_MINUTES (default 20) minutes:
   - gh api /repos/$CI_E2E_REPO/commits/$SHA/check-runs
   - For each of the 11 named required contexts, check that status=completed (not queued/in_progress)
6. Once all 11 have conclusions: assert each has a conclusion field (not empty)
7. Attempt merge: gh pr merge --repo $CI_E2E_REPO <PR> --squash
8. If all passed: assert merge succeeded (exit 0)

### Phase 2 — Synthetic failure + Ruleset block:
1. Create second test branch: e2e-ci-enforcement-fail-$(date +%s)
2. Make a change that will fail at least one check (e.g., introduce a shellcheck error in a .sh file)
3. Push, create PR
4. Poll until at least one required check has conclusion=failure
5. Attempt merge: gh pr merge --repo $CI_E2E_REPO <PR> --squash
6. Assert merge FAILS (gh pr merge exits non-zero) — Ruleset is blocking
7. Cleanup the failing PR/branch

### Required check contexts to verify (read from .github/required-checks.txt at runtime,
### or use a configurable env var CI_E2E_REQUIRED_CHECKS):
The script should read the check names from CI_E2E_REQUIRED_CHECKS env var (space-separated)
or fall back to parsing .github/required-checks.txt (non-comment, non-blank lines).

### Usage comment block at top of file:
```
# Usage:
#   RUN_CI_E2E=1 CI_E2E_REPO=owner/repo bash tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh
# Optional:
#   CI_E2E_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=20  (default: 20)
#   CI_E2E_REQUIRED_CHECKS="check1 check2 ..."  (default: read from .github/required-checks.txt)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh
- [ ] bash tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e-behavioral.sh exits 0 (task 1 behavioral tests pass)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e-behavioral.sh
- [ ] Script exits 0 (SKIP) when RUN_CI_E2E is not set
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh; [ $? -eq 0 ]
- [ ] Script exits non-zero when CI_E2E_REPO is absent with RUN_CI_E2E=1
  Verify: RUN_CI_E2E=1 bash tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] Script contains trap cleanup EXIT for resource cleanup
  Verify: grep -q 'trap cleanup EXIT' tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh
- [ ] Script reads required check contexts (from env var or .github/required-checks.txt)
  Verify: grep -q 'required-checks.txt\|CI_E2E_REQUIRED_CHECKS' tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh
- [ ] ShellCheck passes on the new script
  Verify: shellcheck tests/scripts/test-ci-enforcement-e2e.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
